title: Decision: pkcs11 put() create-before-destroy order must be preserved
description: secretx-pkcs11 put(): collects existing object handles BEFORE creating the new object, creates the new object, then destroys old handles. A contributor might 'simplify' this to destroy-then-create (which is more intuitive). That ordering is wrong: if create_object fails after destroy_object, the secret is gone. The current ordering guarantees: if create fails, existing secret is intact; if destroy fails after create, the new value is live and a duplicate exists (benign). Do not reorder this. The doc comment on put() explains the atomicity invariant; use it to justify the ordering in review.
